channel.title: Miscs on Torgeir Aambø

item.description: A list of the blog posts on my blog

item.title: Other
item.description: Some of my typed up notes, problems and writings.

item.title: Student seminar 2021
item.description: A seminar I arranged during spring 2021.

item.title: Mathematical inspiration week 2021
item.description: A week dedicated to showcasing interesting mathematics the week before the semester starts after summer holiday.

item.title: Lemmarick
item.description:
For fun I have made some limericks centered around the main results from my papers.

Classification of localizing subcategories along $t$-structures

Let $\mathcal{C}$ be stable with a $t$.
As nice as it needs to be.
If the heart has a localization,
then I have a declaration:
There is a unique $\pi$-exact lift to $\mathcal{C}$

Positselski duality in stable $\infty$-categories

Choose a context and take your seat.
I have prepared the following treat.
The hom-tensor adjunction,
makes the following function:
Being contra is equivalent to complete

item.title: Delta-t articles
item.description: A collection of articles I have written for the student newspaper Delta-t.

item.title: About
item.description:
My name is Torgeir Aambø. I’m currently a PhD student at the Norwegian University of Science and Technology (NTNU) in Trondheim, where I am part of the project “Tensor triangulated geometry in Trondheim” supervised by Drew Heard.

This site is meant to host my journey through the three pillars of academia: reading, writing and speaking. Here you will find my projects, talks I have held and been to, various writings, things I read as well as my blog. On the blog you will find posts about what I have been learning (mostly mathematics).

item.title: Have read
item.description: A list of books that I have read.

item.title: NTNU Topology SeminarXiv
item.description:
The SeminarXiv is a bi-weekly seminar in the topology group at NTNU. We choose cool papers and give turns presenting them. We usually meet at 13.00 in room 656.

Schedule

Date Speaker Paper
06.09.24 Marius Nielsen Abstract Goerss–Hopkins theory
20.09.24 Torgeir Aambø Abstract Goerss–Hopkins theory
04.10.24 Trygve Poppe Foundations of Floer homotopy theory I: Flow categories
18.10.24 Alice Hedenlund Foundations of Floer homotopy theory I: Flow categories
01.11.24 Torgeir Aambø Adams spectral sequences and Franke’s algebraicity conjecture
01.11.24 Marius Nielsen A motivic filtration on the topological cyclic homology of commutative ring spectra
29.11.24 Tallak Manum Segalification and the Boardmann-Vogt tensor product
??.??.?? Fredrik Bakke The Cantor-Schröder-Bernstein Theorem for $\infty$-groupoids

item.title: Penguin's great ideas series
item.description: One of Penguin’s wonderful curated list of short books of influential ideas.

item.title: Reading
item.description: I am one of those people that used to read a bit when I was younger, but fell off the reading train somewhere during my teens. During these off-years I still consumed a couple books a year, as well as some essays and writings on the internet, but not in any systematic shape or form. Luckily proper reading found its way back into my life in 2021, in a way stronger than ever before.

item.title: Reading bucket list
item.description: Books I want to read some day.

item.title: Speaking
item.description: Over the years I have grown more and more fond of teaching and speaking, as well as helping other people to learn. I have been fortunate to be able to practise these things during my years of being a student, and to be a part of things while being a PhD student. So, below you will find some lists containing talks, teaching and seminars etc.

item.title: Writing
item.description: One of the things I enjoy the most is writing. I love presenting information and things I know in written form, and I like to think that at least some of it is decently produced. Below you will thus find collection of writing I have done over the years, both in official and unofficial settings. Most of my mathematical writing does however get posted on my blog, which is where I write about the current things I am learning and find interesting.
